Lines.FullText: Structs are immutable objects that contain a set of key-value pairs. The key, as well as the types of each

Elements.RawText: Structs are immutable objects that contain a set of key-value pairs. The key, as well as the types of each

Lines.FullText: associated value for each struct are defined in advance within a Struct type that has the same name as the Struct.

Elements.RawText: associated value for each struct are defined in advance within a Struct type that has the same name as the Struct.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Structs are immutable objects that contain a set of key-value pairs. The key, as well as the types of each
 * associated value for each struct are defined in advance within a Struct type that has the same name as the Struct.

Lines.FullText: When creating a Struct, the Type manager is used to validate the given dictionary of keys and values against the

Elements.RawText: When creating a Struct, the Type manager is used to validate the given dictionary of keys and values against the

Lines.FullText: Struct type stored within the manager. If no type with the given Struct name is currently stored, a default type

Elements.RawText: Struct type stored within the manager. If no type with the given Struct name is currently stored, a default type

Lines.FullText: is created using the Struct field names and values as its parameters. When creating a Struct, fields that are part

Elements.RawText: is created using the Struct field names and values as its parameters. When creating a Struct, fields that are part

Lines.FullText: of the Struct type can be omitted. If so, they will be replaced by either `null` or, if provided by the Struct type,

Elements.RawText: of the Struct type can be omitted. If so, they will be replaced by either `null` or, if provided by the Struct type,

Lines.FullText: the default value of the field.

Elements.RawText: the default value of the field.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * When creating a Struct, the Type manager is used to validate the given dictionary of keys and values against the
 * Struct type stored within the manager. If no type with the given Struct name is currently stored, a default type
 * is created using the Struct field names and values as its parameters. When creating a Struct, fields that are part
 * of the Struct type can be omitted. If so, they will be replaced by either `null` or, if provided by the Struct type,
 * the default value of the field.

Lines.FullText: In the case that a field name is present that is not part of the struct type, or if the value type of the field does

Elements.RawText: In the case that a field name is present that is not part of the struct type, or if the value type of the field does

Lines.FullText: not match, construction of the Struct will fail.

Elements.RawText: not match, construction of the Struct will fail.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * In the case that a field name is present that is not part of the struct type, or if the value type of the field does
 * not match, construction of the Struct will fail.

Lines.FullText: NOTE: Field values of fields with the Core type `ctUndefined` can hold any value, regardless of its type.

Elements.RawText: NOTE: Field values of fields with the Core type `ctUndefined` can hold any value, regardless of its type.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * NOTE: Field values of fields with the Core type `ctUndefined` can hold any value, regardless of its type.

Lines.FullText: Structs are an openDAQ core type (ctStruct). Several objects in openDAQ such as an Unit, or DataDescriptor are Structs,

Elements.RawText: Structs are an openDAQ core type (ctStruct). Several objects in openDAQ such as an Unit, or DataDescriptor are Structs,

Lines.FullText: allowing for access to their fields through Struct methods. Such objects are, by definiton, immutable - their fields

Elements.RawText: allowing for access to their fields through Struct methods. Such objects are, by definiton, immutable - their fields

Lines.FullText: cannot be modified. In order to change the value of a Struct-type object, a new Struct must be created.

Elements.RawText: cannot be modified. In order to change the value of a Struct-type object, a new Struct must be created.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Structs are an openDAQ core type (ctStruct). Several objects in openDAQ such as an Unit, or DataDescriptor are Structs,
 * allowing for access to their fields through Struct methods. Such objects are, by definiton, immutable - their fields
 * cannot be modified. In order to change the value of a Struct-type object, a new Struct must be created.

Lines.FullText: A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,

Elements.RawText: A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,

Lines.FullText: `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned

Elements.RawText: `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned

Lines.FullText: types.

Elements.RawText: types.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,
 * `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned
 * types.

Lines.FullText: The Struct types are stored within a Type manager. In any given instance of openDAQ, a single Type manager should

Elements.RawText: The Struct types are stored within a Type manager. In any given instance of openDAQ, a single Type manager should

Lines.FullText: exist that is part of its Context.

Elements.RawText: exist that is part of its Context.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The Struct types are stored within a Type manager. In any given instance of openDAQ, a single Type manager should
 * exist that is part of its Context.
